name: do
tagline: Orchestrator skill that deploys subagents to execute a phased implementation plan with verification gates before committing.
verdict: Clean, low-risk skill. It only issues orchestration instructions to Claude (deploy subagents, verify, then commit) — no credential access, no network calls, no shell-piped remote code, nothing hidden. Minor polish gaps: no stated negative trigger, no explicit output contract, no version/changelog field, and re-run/idempotency behavior (duplicate commits/push

highlights.summary: No negative-trigger guidance (e.g. when a plan doesn't exist yet, or for trivial single-step tasks) is stated anywhere in the file.
No explicit statement of what the orchestrator's final output/report to the user should look like after all phases complete.
Re-running 'do' on a plan already partially executed isn't addressed — could redeploy Commit/Branch-Sync subagents and produce duplicate commits or pushes.
